questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What shape and color is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always an eight-sided (octagon) red sign, a shape used only for stopping.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to clear the intersection
Stop and stay stopped until it turns green
questions.explanation: A steady yellow warns that the signal is about to turn red, so you should prepare to stop if it is safe.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a flashing red traffic light?
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe
Stop and wait for a green light
Treat it as a yield only
questions.explanation: A flashing red light works like a stop sign: come to a full stop and go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: In Maryland, when may you turn right at a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, when the way is clear and no sign prohibits it
Only if there is a green arrow
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed after a full stop when it is safe, unless a posted sign prohibits it.

questions.prompt: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign indicate?
choices: A warning of a hazard or change in conditions ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs warn drivers of upcoming hazards or changes in the road.

questions.prompt: What color are signs used in construction and work zones?
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and maintenance work zones so drivers slow down and stay alert.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of a two-way road mean?
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangular sign mean?
questions.explanation: The three-sided downward triangle is the yield sign, telling you to give way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means you should:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a full stop, then go
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means caution: slow down and watch for cross traffic before proceeding.

questions.prompt: What do white lane lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction; yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians in the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What should you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the way
questions.explanation: Yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you should:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Stop in the circle to let others in
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to vehicles already circulating in it.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is in a crosswalk, a driver must:
choices: Yield and stop for the pedestrian
Proceed if the light is green
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield and stop for pedestrians crossing in marked or unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal in Maryland?
choices: At least 100 feet before the turn
Only as you begin to turn
questions.explanation: Signal your intention at least 100 feet ahead so other drivers know your plan.

questions.prompt: Who must yield when a vehicle is entering a road from a driveway?
choices: The vehicle entering from the driveway
The traffic already on the road
questions.explanation: A driver entering a roadway from a driveway or private road must yield to traffic already on the road.

questions.prompt: Before making a turn, you should check:
questions.explanation: Always check mirrors and blind spots before turning to avoid cyclists, pedestrians, and other vehicles.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection, which statement is true?
choices: The vehicle that arrives first generally goes first
The faster vehicle always goes first
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, the first to arrive goes first; if simultaneous, yield to the driver on your right.

questions.prompt: Does having a green light give you an absolute right to proceed?
choices: No; you must still yield to pedestrians and vehicles already in the intersection
Yes, as long as you are turning
No; you must always stop first
questions.explanation: A green light means go only when the intersection is clear; you must still yield to those already in it.

questions.prompt: In Maryland, what is the speed limit on undivided highways in a residential district unless otherwise posted?
questions.explanation: Unless posted otherwise, the limit on business-district highways and undivided residential highways is generally 30 mph.

questions.prompt: What is a recommended minimum following distance behind the vehicle ahead?
choices: At least three to four seconds
Ten car lengths regardless of speed
questions.explanation: A following gap of at least three to four seconds gives you time to react and stop safely.

questions.prompt: Speed limits are set for which conditions?
questions.explanation: Posted limits assume ideal conditions; you must slow down when weather or traffic is poor.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance?
choices: In rain, snow, fog, or when following large vehicles
questions.explanation: Poor conditions and large vehicles require extra following distance because stopping takes longer.

questions.prompt: What is the danger of following too closely (tailgating)?
choices: It is a leading cause of rear-end collisions
It is legal at any distance
questions.explanation: Tailgating leaves too little time to stop and is a top cause of rear-end crashes.

questions.prompt: You should never drive faster than:
choices: The speed at which you can stop within the distance you can see ahead
The car in front of you
questions.explanation: Always be able to stop within your sight distance, especially at night or in fog.

questions.prompt: If the posted limit is 55 mph but the road is icy, you should:
choices: Drive slower than the posted limit for safety
Drive faster to get off the road sooner
questions.explanation: You must reduce speed below the posted limit when conditions like ice make the road hazardous.

questions.prompt: Maintaining a space cushion around your vehicle helps you:
choices: Have room to maneuver in an emergency
questions.explanation: Keeping space on all sides gives you room to react and avoid sudden hazards.

questions.prompt: Before entering a curve, you should:
choices: Brake hard in the middle of the curve
questions.explanation: Reduce speed before a curve so you keep control without braking hard while turning.

questions.prompt: When is it unsafe and illegal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a hill or curve where you cannot see far ahead
On a straight road with a broken yellow line
When the passing lane is clearly empty
questions.explanation: Never pass where your view is blocked, such as on hills or curves, because oncoming traffic may be hidden.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is mainly for:
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right; the left lane is intended for passing.

questions.prompt: When may you pass a stopped school bus with red lights flashing and stop arm out?
choices: Only on a divided highway when traveling on the opposite side of a median
Whenever you are in a hurry
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children, except when separated by a median on a divided highway.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rear-view mirror
Only after using your high beams
questions.explanation: Return only after the passed vehicle is visible in your mirror, so you do not cut it off.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check your mirrors and blind spot
questions.explanation: Always signal and check mirrors and blind spots to be sure the lane is clear before moving over.

questions.prompt: When passing, is it acceptable to exceed the speed limit?
choices: No, you must stay within the speed limit
Yes, if the road is clear
questions.explanation: You must not exceed the posted speed limit even to complete a pass.

questions.prompt: How should you change lanes on a multi-lane road?
choices: One lane at a time after signaling and checking it is clear
Without signaling if traffic is light
questions.explanation: Change lanes one at a time and only after signaling and confirming the lane is open.

questions.prompt: A solid white line on a multi-lane road generally means:
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages crossing or changing lanes at that point.

questions.prompt: When driving near large trucks and buses, you should:
choices: Stay out of their blind spots
Cut in front of them sharply
questions.explanation: Large vehicles have big blind spots, so stay where the driver can see you and avoid cutting in close.

questions.prompt: How much clearance must Maryland drivers give when passing a bicyclist?
questions.explanation: Maryland law requires drivers to leave at least three feet of space when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Maryland's Move Over Law requires you to move over or slow down for:
choices: Any stopped vehicle displaying warning signals
questions.explanation: Drivers must move over a lane or slow down for any stopped vehicle showing warning signals when it is safe.

questions.prompt: Why should you look twice for motorcycles at intersections?
choices: They are small and easy to overlook
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and can be hidden in traffic, so look carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: Large trucks making a right turn often:
choices: Swing wide and may need extra room
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to make turns, so give them space and never try to squeeze beside them.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door after parking on a street, you should:
choices: Check for approaching bicyclists and traffic
Open it as fast as possible
questions.explanation: Check for cyclists and traffic before opening a door to avoid a dangerous collision.

questions.prompt: How should you treat a bicyclist riding on the road?
choices: As a legitimate road user with the right to the lane
As an obstacle to be honked at
As someone who must always ride on the sidewalk
As a vehicle you can ignore
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the right to use the road and should be given the same courtesy as other vehicles.

questions.prompt: When you cannot safely move over for a stopped emergency vehicle, you must:
choices: Slow to a safe, reasonable speed
questions.explanation: If moving over is not possible, the Move Over Law requires you to slow to a safe speed as you pass.

questions.prompt: Why do large trucks need more room to stop than cars?
choices: They are much heavier and take longer to stop
questions.explanation: Because of their weight, trucks need far greater stopping distances, so never cut in front and brake suddenly.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older in Maryland?
questions.explanation: A driver 21 or older is over the legal limit at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Maryland's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can be charged at a BAC of:
questions.explanation: Maryland has zero tolerance for underage drinking and driving, with a BAC limit of 0.02 for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: What does Maryland law say about handheld cell phones while driving?
choices: Holding a handheld phone while driving is prohibited
It is allowed at any time
It is allowed only on highways
It is allowed if you drive slowly
questions.explanation: Maryland bans holding a handheld cell phone while operating a vehicle that is in motion.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Maryland is:
choices: A primary offense you can be stopped and cited for
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is a primary offense, so an officer can stop and cite you for it alone.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to sober up before driving?
choices: Allow enough time for the alcohol to leave your body
questions.explanation: Only time can lower your BAC; coffee, showers, and food do not make you sober.

questions.prompt: May a Maryland learner's permit or provisional license holder use a handheld or hands-free phone while driving?
choices: No, except to call 911 in an emergency
questions.explanation: New drivers with a permit or provisional license may not use any wireless device except to call 911 in an emergency.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It impairs judgment, reaction time, and vision
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol reduce judgment, slow reactions, and impair vision.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, what else can impair your ability to drive?
questions.explanation: Many medications, legal or not, can cause drowsiness or slowed reactions that impair driving.

questions.prompt: A driver age 21 or older in Maryland may be charged with driving while impaired (DWI) at a BAC as low as:
questions.explanation: Maryland allows a driving while impaired charge at a BAC of 0.07, below the 0.08 DUI threshold.

questions.prompt: What is the safest choice if you plan to drink alcohol?
choices: Arrange a sober driver or other transportation
questions.explanation: If you drink, do not drive; plan ahead for a sober driver, rideshare, or other safe transportation.

questions.prompt: In Maryland, when must your headlights be on?
choices: Whenever your windshield wipers are in continuous use
questions.explanation: Maryland requires headlights on in poor visibility and whenever your wipers run continuously for rain, snow, or fog.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane on a wet road, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer straight
questions.explanation: When hydroplaning, take your foot off the gas and hold the wheel steady until the tires regain grip.

questions.prompt: In fog, which headlights should you use?
questions.explanation: Use low beams in fog; high beams reflect off the fog and reduce your visibility.

questions.prompt: On icy or snowy roads, you should:
choices: Brake gently and leave extra following distance
Drive at the normal speed limit
questions.explanation: Ice greatly increases stopping distance, so brake gently and keep more space ahead.

questions.prompt: If weather makes driving too dangerous to continue, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Drive faster to finish the trip
questions.explanation: When conditions are too dangerous, pull off the road, use hazard lights, and wait for it to improve.

questions.prompt: How do rain and snow affect your tires' grip on the road?
choices: They reduce traction, so you should slow down
They only affect braking, not steering
questions.explanation: Wet and snowy surfaces reduce traction, so reduce speed to keep control.

questions.prompt: At night, you should drive so that you can stop within:
choices: The distance lit by your headlights
questions.explanation: Drive at a speed that lets you stop within the area your headlights illuminate.

questions.prompt: When are daytime running lights an adequate substitute for headlights in poor weather?
choices: They are not a substitute for headlights
questions.explanation: Daytime running lights do not light the rear of the car, so turn on full headlights in poor weather.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a fire hydrant, on a crosswalk, or in an intersection is prohibited.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing downhill, turn wheels toward the curb so the car rolls into it instead of into traffic.

questions.prompt: In Maryland, a child must ride in an appropriate child safety seat or booster until what age, unless they are at least 4 feet 9 inches tall?
questions.explanation: Children under 8 must use a proper child restraint unless they are at least 4 feet 9 inches tall.

questions.prompt: Maryland requires children under what age to ride in a rear-facing seat unless they exceed its limits?
questions.explanation: Children under 2 must ride rear-facing unless they exceed the seat's height or weight limit.

questions.prompt: Who must wear a seat belt in a vehicle under Maryland law?
questions.explanation: Maryland requires the driver and all passengers to be properly buckled.

questions.prompt: Before driving away, you should make sure your vehicle is:
questions.explanation: Check that your vehicle is safe to drive, including lights, brakes, and tires, before setting out.

questions.prompt: When should you use your turn signals?
choices: For every turn and lane change
Only when other cars are near
questions.explanation: Use turn signals for all turns and lane changes so others can predict your movements.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane road, the general rule is to:
questions.explanation: Keep to the right and use the left lane mainly for passing to keep traffic flowing safely.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner's permit in Maryland?
questions.explanation: You may apply for a Maryland learner's permit at 15 years and 9 months of age.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised practice must a Maryland learner complete?
questions.explanation: Learners must complete 60 hours of supervised driving practice, at least 10 of them at night.

questions.prompt: How long must a Maryland learner's permit be held before taking the skills test?
questions.explanation: The learner's permit must be held for a minimum of 9 months before the provisional skills test.

questions.prompt: Who can serve as a supervising driver for a Maryland learner?
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 with 3 years of driving experience
questions.explanation: A supervising driver must be at least 21 and have held a valid license for at least 3 years.

questions.prompt: Under Maryland's provisional license, a driver under 18 may not drive unsupervised between:
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers under 18 may not drive between midnight and 5 a.m. without a qualified supervisor.

questions.prompt: For the first 151 days of a provisional license, a Maryland driver under 18 may not carry:
choices: Passengers under 18 who are not immediate family
questions.explanation: For the first 151 days, provisional drivers under 18 may not carry under-18 passengers unless they are immediate family.

questions.prompt: While driving with a learner's permit, you must always have:
choices: A qualified supervising driver seated beside you
A friend in the back seat
No one else in the car
questions.explanation: A learner must always have a qualified supervising driver in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: What must you pass to first obtain a Maryland learner's permit?
questions.explanation: To get a learner's permit you must pass the written knowledge test and a vision screening.

questions.prompt: How does Maryland track a driver's traffic violations?
choices: Through a points system on the driving record
By counting tickets only once a year
questions.explanation: Maryland adds points to your record for violations, and too many can lead to suspension or revocation.

questions.prompt: By driving in Maryland, you give implied consent to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs if lawfully stopped
Paying any fine without a hearing
questions.explanation: Implied consent means refusing a lawful alcohol or drug test results in an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you refuse a chemical test after a lawful stop in Maryland?
choices: You receive a discount on fines
questions.explanation: Refusing a lawful chemical test triggers an automatic license suspension under implied consent.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many points on your Maryland record can lead to:
questions.explanation: Too many points can require driver improvement programs or lead to suspension or revocation.

questions.prompt: Speeding in a work zone or school zone in Maryland generally results in:
choices: A lower speed limit being removed
questions.explanation: Work and school zones carry heightened penalties, including larger fines and added points.

questions.prompt: For a new driver, a traffic conviction during the provisional period can:
choices: Extend restrictions and delay a full license
questions.explanation: Convictions during the provisional period can extend restrictions and push back getting a full license.

questions.prompt: A high test result or test refusal for alcohol may require a driver to install:
questions.explanation: A high BAC or a test refusal can require an ignition interlock device that prevents driving after drinking.

questions.prompt: Why should new drivers especially avoid traffic violations?
choices: Violations can delay earning a full license
Violations are free for new drivers
Points do not apply to them
questions.explanation: For new drivers, even minor convictions can extend restrictions and delay full licensing.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Allow a larger following distance than for a car
Drive in the same part of the lane
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly, so leave extra following distance to react safely.

questions.prompt: The first rain after a dry spell can make roads especially slippery because:
choices: Oil and dirt mix with water to reduce traction
questions.explanation: Light rain lifts oil and grime to the surface, making roads very slick until it washes away.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill, turn wheels away from the curb so the car rolls back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: How many of the 60 required supervised practice hours must be at night?
questions.explanation: Of the 60 supervised practice hours, at least 10 must be completed at night.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, Maryland law requires you to:
choices: Stop and exchange information, and report it when required
Only stop if someone is injured
Wait until the next day to report it
questions.explanation: You must stop at a crash, share information, render aid, and report it as required by law.

questions.prompt: What is 'covering the brake' and when is it useful?
choices: Resting your foot over the brake to react faster near hazards
Covering the brake pedal with a mat
questions.explanation: Holding your foot over the brake near hazards shortens reaction time if you must stop.

questions.prompt: A center left-turn lane (two-way left-turn lane) may be used to:
choices: Make left turns from either direction
questions.explanation: The shared center lane marked with yellow lines is only for making left turns, not for through travel or passing.
